title: Ammoseris patens
intro: Ammoseris patens, also known as desert rose or Adam's rib, is a succulent plant species native to Africa, characterized by its unique, sprawling habit and striking pink or white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ammoseris patens include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ammoseris patens is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, making it a valuable resource for treating various ailments in Africa and other parts of the world.
taxonomy: Ammoseris patens is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, which is now considered part of the family Apocynaceae due to the latest taxonomic updates. This plant belongs to the order Gentianales. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it is a genus of flowering plants within the family Apocynaceae. Ammoseris patens is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
